shelfmark: UTO Sinai Georgian NF M 90, SPP No. 325
fol: Reconstructed folio order, ff. 25r, [Georgian NF 84:1v], 25v, [Georgian NF 84:1r], 22r+19v, 22v+19r, 21r+20v, 21v+20r, [Georgian NF 84:5v+4r], [Georgian NF 84:5r+4v], 28v+29r, 28r+29v, [Georgian NF 84:3r+2v], [Georgian NF 84:3v+2r]

note: Fragments do not allow for an exact determination of the number of columns and lines

bib.citation: Shanidze, A. Sinuri Mravaltavi 864 c'lisa. Tbilisi, 1959
Abuladze, I. "Mravaltavi": Enis, ist'oriisa da mat'erialuri k'ult'uris inst'it'ut'is moambe 14. 1944
Van Esbroeck, Michel. Les plus anciens homéliaires géorgiens: Étude descriptive et historique. Louvain-La-Neuve, 1973

cataloguer.message: Description and identification of undertexts as part of SPP (2011-2016). Migrated to SMDL in 2025.

program.label: Sinai Palimpsests Project
program.description: Imaged and studied as part of the Sinai Palimpsests Project (2011–2016), a collaboration between St. Catherine’s Monastery of the Sinai, the Early Manuscripts Electronic Library, and UCLA Library, funded by the Arcadia Fund and the Ahmanson Foundation. The project used multispectral imaging and scholarly analysis to recover and document erased and obscured texts in palimpsest manuscripts from the monastery’s collection.
